channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hearing could set rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 began arguing some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a Wednesday morning hearing.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are dire wolves de-extinct? Genetically engineered pups spark controversy
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the Dominican capital collapsed during a merengue concert, killing at least 124 people and injuring hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nicaragua’s crackdown on the Catholic Church is worsening, exiles and human rights advocates say
item.description: Preparations for popular, often daylong Lent and Holy Week processions are underway across Latin America — but not in Nicaragua. They’ve been largely banned for a second year, one of many concerns for the faithful in a country that human rights advocates, exiled priests and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Republicans are going public with their growing worries about Trump's tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Manufacturers struggling to make long-term plans. Farmers facing retaliation from Chinese buyers. U.S. households burdened with higher prices.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Federal government questioned former Columbia University president over campus antisemitism
item.description: Former Columbia University interim President Katrina Armstrong struggled to answer questions from Department of Health and Human Services investigators over campus antisemitism issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Oklahoma woman accused of laundering $1.5M from elderly victims
item.description: Charges have been filed against Christine Echohawk for laundering about $1.5 million obtained through romance scams, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Sellers/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: New tariffs take effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of tariffs is now in effect and concerns from Capitol Hill are growing louder, even among Republican lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Brandt, Fox 5

item.title: Kennedy’s moves on measles don't quell criticism
item.description: The outbreak represents the first time Kennedy has had to reconcile his past as a leading critic of vaccines and his leadership of the federal health establishment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than 900 illegal aliens charged with immigration-related crimes during first week of April: DOJ
item.description: US attorneys in six districts filed immigration-related charges against nearly 1,000 illegal aliens during the first week of April under Operation Take Back America, the DOJ said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many older AAPI adults are foreign-born. They're tough on immigration too, a new poll finds
item.description: A new poll finds sharp generational disagreements among Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders when it comes to how the U.S. should handle mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Newly released Delphi exhibits include Richard Allen's confession tapes, police interviews
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. – For the first time, the public is hearing convicted Delphi murderer Richard Allen in his own words, including confessions made to his wife while he was held in prison. Trial exhibits showing Allen’s interviews with police on Oct. 13, 2022, and Oct. 26, 2022, have been released.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of deluges overfilled rivers to near-record levels across Kentucky, residents were anxious Tuesday to return to their flooded homes and assess what's salvageable, even as stubbornly high waters kept some of them waiting even longer.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah woman who survived being shot in the head as a teenager now in hospice
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now on hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Murphy, ABC 4

item.title: Arroyo Colorado to be dredged after historic Rio Grande Valley floods
item.description: A Colorado company will design the dredging of a section of a South Texas waterway that flooded during historic recent rains, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commission says.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge in Louisiana said she would terminate the case against Mahmoud Khalil if the government does not provide evidence this week justifying their attempted deportation of the Columbia University student activist.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, KRON4

item.title: Man, 71, arrested after 7 'emotional support' tigers found in his house
item.description: In a twist, Mitchell said he rescued the tigers from Joe Exotic, the Oklahoma zookeeper who was the center of the Netflix series “Tiger King.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump plans steep fines for immigrants who fail to leave after deportation order
item.description: The Trump administration is planning to fine migrants up to $998 a day if they fail to leave after being put under deportation orders, according to a report from Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: California man arrested near Justice Kavanaugh's home with a gun pleads guilty to attempted murder
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A California man pleaded guilty on Tuesday to trying to kill U.S. Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh at his home in a suburb of Washington, D.C., nearly three years ago. Nicholas John Roske was arrested near Kavanaugh’s home in Chevy Chase, Maryland, in June 2022.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: S&P 500 rises 3.4% as some relief washes through financial markets worldwide
item.description: Stocks are rising on Wall Street and around the world to recover some of the historic losses since last week when President Donald Trump announced sharp increases in taxes on goods imported to the U.S. The S&P 500 was up 3.4% in early trading Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Federal magistrate weighs arguments in Idaho news groups' execution access lawsuit
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A federal magistrate judge is expected to hear arguments Tuesday morning in a lawsuit brought by three news organizations that say Idaho prison officials are unconstitutionally hiding parts of lethal injection executions from public view.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's DHS revokes legal status for migrants who entered the US on Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: MCALLEN, Texas (AP) — Migrants who were temporarily allowed to live in the United States by using a Biden-era online appointment app have been told to leave the country “immediately,” officials said Monday. It was unclear how many beneficiaries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah Red Cross volunteers deployed to assist with flooding, tornado relief in several other states
item.description: Volunteers with the American Red Cross of Utah disaster team have been deployed to several states that have been affected by recent tornadoes and flooding in the Midwest and South.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to deport Venezuelans under wartime law, but only after judges' review
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday allowed the Trump administration to use an 18th century wartime law to deport Venezuelan migrants, but said they must get a court hearing before they are taken from the United States.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KTLA 5
